name: French Defense
description: A strategic defense built on a rock-solid pawn chain. Black accepts a constrained light-squared bishop in exchange for central stability and clear attacking plans on the queenside. The Winawer variation leads to some of the most unbalanced play in chess.

moves.instruction: Stake the king's pawn claim in the center of the board.
The French Defense. Prepare ...d5 with solid pawn support.
Build a strong pawn duo in the center, the natural response.
Strike at e4. Black challenges the center head-on.
Defend e4 and develop. The most principled reply.
The Winawer. Pin the knight — Black removes a key defender of e4.
Push forward. White accepts a structural cost to gain space and the initiative.
Undermine the d4-pawn, which has been left in need of support.
Question the bishop. White is willing to accept doubled pawns for the bishop pair.
Capture and give White doubled c-pawns — Black gets the bishop pair, White gets a center.
Recapture toward the center. White now has a massive pawn chain on c3-d4-e5.
Continue undermining — target the base of White's chain at d4.
Develop and reinforce d4, the critical point of the structure.
Add another attacker to d4. The pressure on White's center is mounting.
Develop the bishop and prepare to castle into safety.
Place the queen actively, defending the d6 break idea and pressuring the e-file.
